summarization.a: The figure displays a spectra chart comparing the intensity of Co3O4 control, and the initial Co3O4 powder after 15 Hacac exposures at 250 °C.

data_extraction.a:
| 2 Theta (degrees) | Co3O4 after Hacac | Co3O4 control |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 30 | peak at 32°| peak at 32° |
| 35 | peak at 37°, 39° | peak at 37° |
| 40 | peak at 43° | peak at 43° |
| 45 | peak at 45° | - |
| 50 | - | - |
| 55 | peak at 56° | peak at 56° |
| 60 | peak at 60°, 62° | peak at 60° |
| 65 | peak at 66° | peak at 6° |
| 70 | - | - |

a.question: Which crystal planes can be seen in the figure?
a.answer: c_CoO crystal planes: (200) and (220), and c-Co3O4 crystal planes: (220), (311), (222), (400), (422), (511) and (440).

a.question: Which species can be observed on the initial CoO control?
Which crystal planes can be observed after 15 Hacac exposures?
a.answer: (200) and (220) crystal planes, which correspond to c-CoO, and (220), (311), (222), (400), (422), (511) and (440), which correspond to c-Co3O4.

a.question: What effect does the 15 Hacac exposures have on the oxidation of the control?
a.answer: After 15 Hacac exposures, part of the c-Co3O4 turns into c-CoO.
